perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall pay to Fox, or such other party as Fox may designate in writing, a royalty in the following amount: (a) From the first unit sold, Fox shall earn a royalty at the rate of ***** percent (*****%) of Licensee's Gross Receipts (as defined in the Agreement) from Licensee's sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products. ("IA2 Audio and Video Royalty").
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall pay to Fox, or such other party as Fox may designate in writing, a royalty in an amount to be determined based on market conditions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific royalty rate (*****%), the modified text introduces ambiguity regarding how the royalty amount will be calculated. While it still mentions a royalty payment, the absence of a defined rate contradicts the implicitly defined framework for financial obligations within the 'Agreement', creating uncertainty in enforcement of royalty payments. The market conditions will be the only parameter to define the royalty.
perturbation.location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 1
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 1
perturbation.contradicted_text: Royalty: In consideration of the rights granted to Licensee pursuant to this Amendment 2, Licensee shall pay to Fox, or such other party as Fox may designate in writing, a royalty in an amount to be determined based on market conditions. For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee may not recoup any portion of the Ice Age 2 Guarantee (as defined in Amendment 1) through its sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a royalty rate as a percentage of gross receipts, providing a clear method for calculating the royalty. The changed text replaces this specific rate with 'an amount to be determined based on market conditions.' This contradicts the contradicted text, which reiterates the 'market conditions' basis for determining the royalty, as it removes the previously defined and quantifiable method for calculating royalties, creating a significant ambiguity and potential for disputes regarding the amount owed. The omission of the rate directly conflicts with any expectation of a fixed or formula-based royalty, leading to an in-text contradiction according to the definition of Omissions – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA Audio and Video Wireless Products shall be treated in the same manner as set forth in Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA Audio and Video Wireless Products shall be subject to negotiation.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the clear directive of following Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the 'Agreement' with 'subject to negotiation,' the modification introduces uncertainty. It contradicts the intention of the original agreement which was to treat those payments as explicitly defined in the agreement, creating ambiguity that can be used to exploit the agreement during the payments to Fox Mobile Entertainment, Inc.
perturbation.location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 2
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 2
perturbation.contradicted_text: VGSL and other CSP Accounting: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA Audio and Video Wireless Products shall be subject to negotiation. ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that VGSL and other CSP payments would be treated in the same manner as specified in Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the Agreement, providing a clear guideline for payment processing. However, the changed text replaces this directive with 'subject to negotiation,' directly contradicting the initial intent of fixed treatment according to specified paragraphs. This introduction of negotiability creates ambiguity and directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which repeats the changed text that payments are 'subject to negotiation'. This means the payments, which were previously fixed, are now subjected to uncertainty because of the negotiation terms. The introduction of the word 'negotiation' directly breaks with the initial agreement, and leads to a potential legal issue within the document

perturbation.original_text: Except as herein expressly amended or by necessary implication modified by this Amendment, the Agreement in all other respects is hereby ratified and shall continue in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agreement is ratified and continues in effect, subject to ongoing review and potential future amendments.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text adds 'subject to ongoing review and potential future amendments', which undermines the original clause that the agreement is ratified and continues in effect. The original clause confirms the agreement unless otherwise amended in this document explicitly. Adding the text 'subject to ongoing review and potential future amendments' generates ambiguity and weakens the binding nature of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Direct-to-Consumer Distribution Royalty: Licensee shall not be entitled to receive a share of any revenues and shall have no claim to any revenues earned and collected by Fox, or a third-party Fox designates, for the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products which Fox distributes through its and its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels. Except as herein expressly amended or by necessary implication modified by this Amendment, The Agreement is ratified and continues in effect, subject to ongoing review and potential future amendments. By signing in the places indicated below, the parties hereto accept and agree to all of the terms and conditions hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement continues in full force and effect except as expressly amended. The changed text adds 'subject to ongoing review and potential future amendments,' which introduces uncertainty and undermines the ratified nature of the agreement. The contradicted text includes both the 'full force and effect' clause and a specific 'No Direct-to-Consumer Distribution Royalty' clause. Because the changed text introduces the possibility of future amendments outside of the scope of the agreement's existing amendment procedures, it creates ambiguity and makes it unclear whether the 'No Direct-to-Consumer Distribution Royalty' clause will be upheld.
